title: cout - printing in C++
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: C++ prints with std::cout and the << operator, and you can chain values together.
explanation: cout is the standard output stream. Each << sends one more thing to it, so you can line up text, variables, and endl (a newline) in a single row.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: << sends each piece to the screen in turn.
The text, then the value of year.

exercises.question: Which operator sends a value to cout?

Which keyword adds a newline at the end of a cout line?

std::cout << year << std::_;
